page: Amazon Web Services c7i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom 4th Generation Intel Xeon Scalable processors (model 8488C) running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 192 virtual CPUs mapped to 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 384 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a compute-centric memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2 GB per vCPU. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides 50 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. There is no local NVMe storage or GPU hardware bundled with this configuration.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier (top 10%) for multi-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests. The processor's support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) drives top-tier performance in specialized workloads like cryptography, ray tracing, and image processing. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached read-only small block operations and uncached large block transfers both scoring in the top 10%. However, single-core performance and overall memory latency metrics fall into the average tier, indicating that the instance's strength lies in highly parallelized execution rather than single-threaded speed.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7i.48xlarge offers high resource density for compute-bound workloads, leveraging DDR5 memory and dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of local storage. It is highly suited for compute-intensive tasks such as high-performance computing (HPC), batch processing, scientific modeling, and distributed analytics. The presence of AMX instructions also makes it a viable option for CPU-based machine learning and LLM inference, where it achieves strong performance in prompt processing and text generation across various model sizes. It is also well-suited for high-throughput static web serving and large-scale Redis deployments.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processors. It is equipped with 384 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 50 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic execution, driven by hardware-accelerated AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets. While single-core performance is average, its massive parallel processing capabilities make it highly efficient for compute-heavy workloads. The instance is optimized for high-performance computing, batch processing, distributed analytics, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring 192 vCPUs and DDR5 memory for highly parallelized, processor-intensive enterprise workloads.

384 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic performance
Hardware acceleration via Intel AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
